scope.summary: Explicitly wire the rigid trim simulation step (MT-169, parry3d rigid body step) into the sim-over-timeline loop (MT-289) so each animated frame advances both the cloth XPBD pass and the trim rigid-body pass, with two-way coupling preserved across frames.
acceptance_criteria: The sim-over-timeline frame advance function calls both the cloth XPBD substep loop and the rigid trim step for each animated frame, in the correct order (cloth predict -> rigid step -> coupling -> cloth project -> finalize).
Per-frame mesh cache (MT-290) stores rigid trim world positions alongside cloth particle positions for each cached frame.
The TRIM_ATTACHED ValidationDescriptor (MT-189) is applied across a multi-frame animation run, not only at static fit end.
An integration test simulates a jacket with buttons through a 30-frame walk cycle and asserts TRIM_ATTACHED holds at every frame.
scope.risk_if_missed: Animated garments with hardware regress silently: trims either freeze at their fitted position or detach because the rigid step is omitted from the timeline loop. The per-frame cache is incomplete (trim positions missing), breaking downstream DCC reconstruction of animated hardware.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
